name: Claude Code — product context and compaction
context_primitives: Terminal/IDE coding agent with repo tools, CLAUDE.md project instructions, MCP, and **auto-compaction** near context pressure (widely documented in community analyses aligned with Anthropic API compaction). DeLucia stated publicly that **Claude Code** discussions describe a **similar head/tail truncation + compression** pattern to Alex—convergent industry practice without identical implementation details in her talk.
deployment_pattern: CLAUDE.md for stable rules; MCP for external data; rely on auto-compact or manual /compact at task boundaries; subagent patterns in newer SDK docs.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: No public Alex-style long-session eval spec; SWE-bench style coding leaderboards measure end tasks not context policy.
ecosystem_integrations: Anthropic models, MCP, git workspaces, CI.
differentiators: vs. **Alex**: Claude Code optimizes **general coding**; Alex optimizes **observability trace analysis** where context is **telemetry-heavy** and failures feed back into spans. Both converge on **truncate/compress + don't reset system prompt + delegate heavy work**.
significance_for_builders: Validates that **custom Alex-style heuristics** are not exotic—major coding agents ship comparable context survival tactics; your differentiator is **domain-specific evals** (10-turn → 11th-turn) and **retrieval IDs** for dropped middle.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Claude Code GA evolution 2025–2026; compaction API alignment per Anthropic docs.

primary_sources.title: DeLucia talk — Claude Code similarity (user transcript)
